RuleSet.Name: src/schemapubobj/IPubEditObj.java

GenFile.Name: reallyFileIPubEditObjJava
GenFile.BasePackageName: $reference ManufacturingSchema lower JavaPackage$.$reference ManufacturingSchema lower SchemaName$pub/src/main/java
GenFile.SubPackageName: $reference ManufacturingSchema lower JavaPackage$.$reference ManufacturingSchema lower SchemaName$pubobj

RuleSet.GenRule: public void copyRecToOrig(); public void copyOrigToRec();
, I$reference SuperClassRelation reference ToTable SchemaName$Pub$reference SuperClassRelation reference ToTable TableName$EditObj
$switch RelationTypeTag Superclass empty Components declIPubEditObjRelationSingletonGetter Children declIPubEditObjRelationSingletonGetter default declIPubEditObjRelationSingletonAccessors$

$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIPubObjColumnBasePKeyGetter default declIPubObjColumnBaseDataGetter$$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIPubObjColumnBasePKeySetter default declIPubObjColumnBaseDataSetter$

$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIPubObjColumnBasePKeyGetter default declIPubObjColumnBaseDataGetter$$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIPubObjColumnBasePKeySetter default declIPubObjColumnBaseDataSetter$

/* * create() may return a different instance than the * one used to invoke the operation. All future references * should be to the returned instance, not the original * invoker. You should lose all references to the original * invoker. * * @return The created instance. */ I$reference BaseClass DefSchemaName$Pub$reference BaseClass TableName$Obj create();
